response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a capable full-stack software craftsman with genuine distributed-systems exposure and a strong TDD/mentoring track record, but the resume falls critically short on the two hardest minimum qualifications: 8 years of C++ (the candidate shows roughly 6 months at National Instruments plus a thesis project) and 5 years of dedicated large-scale infrastructure or cloud platform work. His career arc is firmly in web-application and developer-tooling territory, with primary depth in TypeScript/JavaScript/DLang rather than C++, and AWS CDK usage at Optilyz is pricing-layer application work rather than cloud compute platform ownership.
strengths: Consistent software-craftsmanship discipline (TDD, trunk-based development, CI/CD, pairing) demonstrated across multiple employers
Proven knowledge-sharing and mentoring through conference talks, full-day workshops, and recurring code-kata sessions

Hands-on high-throughput distributed-systems experience at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic, working on micro-services infrastructure handling millions of HTTP requests per second with low-latency, async I/O design
concerns: C++ experience is minimal (~6 months at National Instruments writing CVI components plus a thesis project) versus the mandatory 8-year requirement — the most critical gap for this role

No evidence of owning or designing cloud compute platform infrastructure at scale; Kubernetes usage is limited to deploying an open-source side project, and AWS CDK work is application-level pricing logic
No technical leadership over cloud or infrastructure teams, which is explicitly listed as a preferred qualifier and implied throughout the role's responsibilities
